# sizmo

> Unofficial GoHighLevel CLI for humans and AI agents. Read your CRM, snapshot your location, diff what changed, and make confirm-gated writes — all from the terminal. Zero runtime dependencies. MIT.

Not affiliated with HighLevel. Requires Node.js 22+.

## Install

```
npx sizmo brief              # run without installing
npm install -g sizmo         # install globally
```

First-time setup (guided):

```
sizmo init
```

Or manually:

```
echo "pit-yourtoken..." | sizmo config set --profile myaccount --loc YOUR_LOCATION_ID --pit-stdin
```

PIT = Private Integration Token. Found in GoHighLevel → Settings → Integrations → Private Integrations. Never pass the token as a CLI argument — always pipe via stdin.

## Auth

```
sizmo doctor                 # full health check: scopes, location, version
sizmo auth status            # credential source, location, PIT age
sizmo auth check             # probe live API — verify scopes per lane
```

Multi-client (agency/subaccount use):

```
sizmo config set --profile clientA --loc LOC_A --pit-stdin
sizmo config set --profile clientB --loc LOC_B --pit-stdin
sizmo brief --profile clientA
sizmo brief --profile clientB
```

Profile env: `SIZMO_PROFILE=<name>` — mirrors `AWS_PROFILE`. Precedence: `--profile` flag > env > saved default.

## Read Commands (no confirmation, no risk)

```
sizmo brief                  # morning readout: revenue, unpaid invoices, waiting contacts, stuck deals, action items
sizmo snapshot               # 6-metric card: leads, bookings, show-rate, collected, reply-rate, pipeline value
sizmo triage                 # who's waiting on a reply, longest first
sizmo pipeline               # pipeline health: value by stage + stuck deal sweep
sizmo receivables            # A/R: who owes, how much, how old
sizmo reconcile              # money collected by source + flags
sizmo booked-not-paid        # sessions with no invoice or payment (the money leak)
sizmo noshow                 # no-shows to rebook
sizmo focus                  # one ranked to-do queue by money at stake
sizmo segment                # find contacts by criteria (tag, phone, age, etc.)
sizmo crm                    # query local CRM model: pipelines, calendars, tags, fields, users
sizmo sync                   # refresh local CRM model (run after changing stages or calendars)
sizmo export --out file.json # dump full location structure to one deterministic, diffable JSON file
sizmo diff file.json         # compare saved file vs live — what changed?
sizmo diff a.json b.json     # compare two saved exports
```

`sizmo brief` never invents a number. A blocked data source is reported as unknown, never as zero.

`sizmo export` captures: pipelines + stages, calendars, custom fields, custom values, tags, users, location settings. Deterministic (key-sorted) — byte-identical re-exports unless something actually changed.

`sizmo diff` output is plain English — no IDs, no JSON arrows:
```
Pipelines
  + Renewals
  ~ Sales  —  stages updated

Custom Values
  ~ Booking Link  —  value updated

Tags
  − cold-lead
```

## Write Commands (all require --confirm)

Every write prints a preview and exits 5 without `--confirm`. Nothing fires silently. The PIT scope is the gate — a missing scope fails with AUTH + the exact setting to add.

```
# Contacts
sizmo contact create --email a@b.co --name "Name" --confirm
sizmo contact upsert --email a@b.co --name "Name" --confirm    # de-dupes on email or phone
sizmo contact delete <contactId> --confirm

# Tags and notes
sizmo tag <contactId> --add vip --confirm
sizmo tag <contactId> --remove vip --confirm
sizmo note <contactId> --text "Called, interested" --confirm

# Opportunities
sizmo opp create --name "Deal" --pipeline "Sales" --stage "New Lead" --contact <id> --confirm
sizmo opp move <oppId> --stage "Proposal Sent" --confirm
sizmo opp update <oppId> --value 50000 --confirm

# Appointments
sizmo appointment book --calendar "Discovery Calls" --contact <id> --start "2026-07-10T09:00" --confirm
sizmo appointment cancel <apptId> --confirm

# Messaging
sizmo send <contactId> --channel sms --message "Hi Ana, checking in" --confirm
sizmo send <contactId> --channel email --message "Hi Ana" --confirm

# Build / scaffold
sizmo field create --name "Coaching Goal" --type TEXT --confirm
sizmo field delete <fieldId> --confirm
sizmo value create --name "Booking Link" --value "https://..." --confirm
sizmo value update <valueId> --value "https://..." --confirm
sizmo value delete <valueId> --confirm
sizmo calendar create --name "Discovery Calls" --confirm
sizmo calendar delete <calendarId> --confirm

# Invoices (scope-gated)
sizmo invoice draft --contact <id> --item "Session:5000" --confirm   # creates document, no charge
sizmo invoice send <invoiceId> --confirm                              # sends pay-link, not a card charge
```

Delete commands are single-target by design. No `--all`, no wildcard. The CLI fetches and names the resource before deleting — a wrong ID returns NOTFOUND, nothing touched.

Money never moves automatically. No card-charging command exists (GoHighLevel exposes no public endpoint for it).

## Confirm Gate Pattern (for agents)

```
# Step 1 — preview (no --confirm): prints change + rerun command, exits 5
sizmo tag ct-001 --add vip --json

# Step 2 — execute (with --confirm): fires the write, exits 0
sizmo tag ct-001 --add vip --confirm

# --dry-run: shows change description without executing, exits 0
sizmo tag ct-001 --add vip --dry-run
```

Agent pattern: call without `--confirm` first. Show the preview to the human. Only call with `--confirm` after explicit human approval.

## JSON Envelope (stable contract)

Every command supports `--json`. Shape is frozen under semver:

```json
{
  "schemaVersion": 1,
  "command": "brief",
  "location": "LOC_ID",
  "data": {},
  "degraded": false,
  "warnings": [],
  "cacheAgeMs": 0
}
```

`degraded: true` = at least one source was blocked. Read `warnings[]`. A blocked source is never zero — treat as unknown.

`--ndjson`: one meta line + one JSON object per list item. Process rows without buffering. Combine with `--fields` to trim payload.

Token-lean flags for agents:
- `--fields a,b,c` — keep only these keys per list item (~80-90% smaller payload)
- `--concise` — leaner brief payload
- `--fresh` — bypass 60-second read cache

```
sizmo receivables --json --fields name,due    # minimal payload
sizmo brief --json --concise                  # trimmed brief
```

## CRM Model (local cache)

sizmo caches slow-changing structure: pipelines + stages, calendars, tags, custom fields, users, location. Recipes resolve names to IDs from this cache without a live fetch.

```
sizmo crm                    # overview: counts + age per entity
sizmo crm pipelines          # list pipelines + stages
sizmo crm tags               # list tags
sizmo sync                   # full refresh
sizmo sync tags              # refresh one entity
```

Staleness is always visible. The model never auto-syncs silently.

## Exit Codes

| Code | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| 0 | OK |
| 1 | API error |
| 2 | Usage error |
| 3 | Auth error / no location |
| 4 | Not found |
| 5 | Confirmation required — rerun with --confirm |

Branch on exit codes, not on output parsing.

## Safety Model

- Reads never change GoHighLevel data
- Writes require explicit `--confirm` — nothing fires silently
- PIT never in argv — stdin or env var only
- Zero runtime dependencies — auditable in an afternoon
- 60-second read cache — use `--fresh` to bypass
- No card-charging command (no public endpoint exists)

## Honest Limitations

- No workflow writes — workflow authoring stays in the GHL UI
- Calendar events truncate at 100 (GHL API has no cursor) — `degraded: true` emitted when hit
- Pipeline monetary values carry no currency field — cross-currency totals are never summed
- Rate limit: 5 concurrent requests max

## Key Files

- [README.md](README.md) — full documentation
- [SKILL.md](SKILL.md) — Claude Code skill (copy to ~/.claude/skills/sizmo-cli.md, load with /sizmo-cli)
- [API-STABILITY.md](API-STABILITY.md) — frozen contract: exit codes, JSON shapes, flag names
- [SECURITY.md](SECURITY.md) — threat model, self-audit recipes, vulnerability reporting
- [CHANGELOG.md](CHANGELOG.md) — version history
- [examples/demo/](examples/demo/) — synthetic demo: before.json, after.json, showcase.gif

## Machine-readable command tree

```
sizmo schema --json
```

Returns the full command tree with flags, types, and descriptions. Feed this to any LLM to understand the complete API surface without reading prose.
